Smuggler's Hold
What Is Smuggler's Hold?
A two-player head-to-head card game — Sabacc meets Cribbage, played on a Dejarik holochess board. You and your droid adversary ID-10T take turns laying cards, racing to score combinations across two distinct phases each round. First to 121 points wins. Expect several rounds of play, each one ratcheting up the pressure as your pieces spiral toward the Inner Core.
The 62-Card Sabacc Deck
Three suits — Coins (●), Flasks (■), Sabers (▲) — each running from +1 to +10 and −1 to −10. That's 60 suited cards, plus two Sylops (value zero). The Sylop is the deck's wild card: it matches any suit, can bridge a positive-to-negative run, and doubles or triples scoring combinations in the Spoils phase. There are only two in the entire deck. If you draw one — you'll know.
The Coruscant Shift Dice
Two peculiar six-sided dice are rolled once per round after hands are chosen, establishing the scoring targets for that entire round.

Number Die — faces: −10 · −5 · 0 · 0 · +5 · +10. Note the two zeroes — this is intentional, making zero a meaningfully higher-probability target. A zero roll changes the entire strategic calculus.

Suit Die — faces: Coins · Coins · Flasks · Flasks · Sabers · Sabers. Each suit appears twice, paired. The rolled suit determines which cards score bonus points in the Spoils phase. Both dice matter all round — keep them visible.
Two Phases Per Round
Each round has two phases played back to back.

Phase 1 — Skirmish: Players alternate laying cards face-up, building a running sum. Score points when the sum hits zero (Sabacc), the dice target number, or exactly ±15 (Outer Rim). Scoring triggers have different consequences — Outer Rim captures all face-up cards and resets the board. The player with the fewest total cards at the end claims the Hold (the discarded cards from the opening deal).

Phase 2 — Spoils (The Count): Each player selects 4 cards from their pool — captured Skirmish cards, the Hold if they won it — plus the shared Path card as a fifth. Score combinations: opposing pairs, runs, suit matches, sum to dice target. Sylops amplify everything they touch.
Key Scoring Moments
ConditionPhasePts
Sum = 0 (Sabacc)Skirmish6 + go again
Sum = Dice #Skirmish3
Sum = ±15 (Outer Rim)Skirmish3 + capture all
Run of 3/4/5 cardsEither3/4/5
Flush — 3/4/5 Dice Suit cardsEither3/4/5
Opposing Pair (Spoils)Spoils6 (+6 per Sylop)
Pure Sabacc (2 Sylops)Spoils12
Kessel Run (last card = Sylop at ±15)Skirmish60
ID-10T & What to Expect
ID-10T is your droid opponent — and, in easier modes, your tutor. His commentary is dry, precise, and occasionally pained. He plays a full strategic game and will not let you win out of courtesy.

On launch: select a difficulty, tap Punch It, and a round begins automatically. ID-10T deals. The Path card sets your opening sum. The dice roll. Then it's your move. The hint button (on Easy and Average) will explain what's worth doing and why — ID-10T narrates it either way. A full game runs 4–8 rounds on average. 121 points wins. Good luck.
